article_9_risk_management.risk_identification_methodology: Multi-agent adversarial debate with structured critique phases. Risk identification emerges from agent disagreement, dissent, and confidence calibration across heterogeneous model ensemble.
foreseeable_misuse_scenarios: Use for irreversible decisions without human review

risk_mitigation_measures.measure: Multi-agent adversarial debate with dissent capture
Heterogeneous model ensemble (different providers and RLHF targets)

residual_risks.description: Correlated model failures on shared blind spots
residual_risks.rationale: Heterogeneous ensemble reduces but does not eliminate shared failures

article_9_risk_management.post_market_monitoring_plan: Periodic re-evaluation via SettlementTracker (automated data checks: days, human review panels: months, market resolution: years). ELO calibration tracks model performance over time. Brier scores updated after settlement.

article_10_data_governance.title: Data and Data Governance
data_quality_measures: Provenance chain with timestamped event log
Knowledge Mound validation with contradiction detection
bias_detection_methods: Heterogeneous model ensemble (different providers reduce shared biases)
Adversarial debate structure surfaces conflicting viewpoints
Dissent capture ensures minority perspectives are recorded
article_10_data_governance.training_data_provenance: Multi-model ensemble: each model trained independently by its provider. No direct training data access or modification by the Aragora platform.
article_10_data_governance.data_governance_policy: Data processed through Aragora decision pipeline with provenance tracking. All inputs hashed for integrity. Knowledge Mound provides validation feedback, contradiction detection, and confidence decay for temporal data quality.
compliance_notes: No explicit data sources declared; recommend documenting input data origins.

article_11_technical_documentation.title: Technical Documentation
article_11_technical_documentation.system_description: Aragora Decision Integrity Platform v2.9.0: Multi-agent adversarial debate platform for decision integrity. Provider: Synaptent / Aragora.
article_11_technical_documentation.development_process: Developed using test-driven methodology with 208,000+ automated tests. Continuous integration with adversarial stress testing (Gauntlet). Multi-model heterogeneous ensemble reduces single-provider failure modes.
monitoring_capabilities: Brier calibration scores for confidence accuracy
Structured audit logging with provenance chain
compliance_notes: No configuration metadata in receipt; recommend including protocol settings.
Low confidence score; review system configuration and agent selection.

input_record.input_summary: Should Aragora prioritize the EU AI Act compliance pipeline over new vertical onboarding for the August 2026 deadline?

annex_iv_sec1_general.intended_purpose: Multi-agent adversarial vetting of decisions against organizational knowledge, delivering audit-ready decision receipts.

retention_policy.basis: Art. 26(6) — minimum 6 months for high-risk systems

article_13_transparency.title: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers

intended_purpose.description: Aragora orchestrates adversarial debate among heterogeneous AI models to vet decisions against organizational knowledge. It produces audit-ready decision receipts with cryptographic integrity for regulatory compliance.
not_intended_for: Fully autonomous decision-making without human oversight

known_risks.description: Plausible but incorrect claims persisting through consensus

output_interpretation.dissent_significance: 2 dissenting view(s) recorded. Review dissenting reasoning before finalizing.

oversight_model.description: System operates with monitoring-based oversight. Human intervention on anomalies.

limitations_documented: Underlying model knowledge cutoff dates apply

mechanisms: Dissent views prominently displayed alongside verdict
Confidence scores presented with interpretation context

explainability: Factor decomposition: contributing factors with weights
Evidence chain: claims linked to sources
Vote pivot: which arguments changed outcomes

mechanisms.description: Deployer rejects AI consensus and decides independently
Previously accepted decisions can be reversed
Halts debate mid-round, partial results preserved
Cancels in-progress decision, no downstream actions

article_15_accuracy_robustness.continuous_monitoring: ELO-based model performance tracking updated after each debate. Brier calibration scores updated after settlement resolution. SettlementTracker monitors claim accuracy over review_horizon_days.

article_43_conformity_assessment.title: Conformity Assessment
findings: No critical or high-severity risks identified
compliance_notes: Internal conformity assessment per Art. 43(2). Third-party assessment may be required for certain Annex III categories.
